name: example
description: TEMPLATE: Replace 'example'/'Example'/'EXAMPLE' with your service name throughout this file and throughout the entire plugin directory. This plugin connects Claude Code to the Example MCP server — a Rust-based MCP server built from rmcp-template.

use_docker.title: Deploy with Docker
use_docker.description: TEMPLATE: Keep this — both Docker and systemd deployment modes are useful defaults. True: run via docker compose (containerized, easier to upgrade, auto-restart). False: run as a systemd user service (lighter weight, no Docker dependency, logs to journald). Both produce identical behavior — pick based on your host conventions.

server_url.title: Server URL
server_url.description: Base URL of the MCP HTTP server this Claude Code session connects to. If running locally: keep http://localhost:40060. If connecting to a remote server: set to http://hostname:40060 or https://example.yourdomain.com. Do NOT include a trailing /mcp — the plugin appends it automatically.

api_token.title: API token
api_token.description: Bearer token for MCP HTTP authentication. Every request to /mcp includes: Authorization: Bearer <token>. Server mode: pick any value and use the same value in EXAMPLE_MCP_TOKEN. Client mode: paste the token your server admin configured. Generate one with: openssl rand -hex 32 or just gen-token.

no_auth.title: Disable service auth
no_auth.description: Run the MCP server without authentication. ONLY safe when the server is bound to 127.x (loopback). Use this for local development only — never expose an unauthed MCP server on a network interface.

auth_mode.title: Auth mode
auth_mode.description: Server auth mode. 'bearer' uses only the static API token. 'oauth' enables Google OAuth/JWT for clients like Codex while the static token remains accepted for this Claude Code plugin connection. OAuth mode requires public_url, google_client_id, google_client_secret, and auth_admin_email.

public_url.title: Public URL
public_url.description: Public base URL for OAuth issuer metadata, e.g. https://example.yourdomain.com. Required when auth_mode=oauth. If empty, setup derives it from an https server_url by stripping /mcp suffix. Server mode only.

google_client_id.title: Google OAuth client ID
google_client_id.description: Google OAuth client ID used when auth_mode=oauth. Create a Web application OAuth client in Google Cloud Console (console.cloud.google.com). The plugin setup script adds the required redirect URIs automatically. Server mode only.

google_client_secret.title: Google OAuth client secret
google_client_secret.description: Google OAuth client secret from the same Google Cloud Console credential. Stored in the generated plugin env file with mode 600 (owner-read-only). Server mode only.

auth_admin_email.title: OAuth admin email
auth_admin_email.description: Bootstrap allowed Google account for OAuth mode. The server refuses to start OAuth mode without at least one allowlisted email address. Server mode only.

example_api_url.title: Service API URL
example_api_url.description: TEMPLATE: Replace 'example_api_url' with your service's credential field name (e.g. 'myservice_api_url'). Your service's API endpoint — the URL this MCP server calls to fetch data. Example: https://api.example.com/v1. Maps to EXAMPLE_API_URL env var.

example_api_key.title: Service API key
example_api_key.description: TEMPLATE: Replace 'example_api_key' with your service's credential field name (e.g. 'myservice_api_key'). API key or bearer token for authenticating to your upstream service. Maps to EXAMPLE_API_KEY env var. Never stored in config.toml.
